title: Intricate Light Turkish Eggplant Imam Bayildi
description: A rich and light Turkish dish featuring nuts and eggplant, made in a cast-iron skillet and suitable for a satvik-friendly diet.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Heat the olive oil in a cast-iron skillet over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the chopped almonds and cook for 2 minutes, stirring frequently.
Add the brine-dried eggplant, salt, black pepper, and smoked paprika, and stir to combine.
Add the concentrated tomato paste and stir to combine, cooking for an additional 2 minutes.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 25 minutes, or until the eggplant is tender and lightly browned.
Remove the skillet from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
Serve the Intricate Light Turkish Eggplant Imam Bayildi hot, garnished with additional chopped almonds if desired.
